paper_title: Parsimony Analysis of Old World Rhinos

methods.description: This method involves conducting a parsimony analysis on 74 taxa of Old World rhinos using the TNT software. The analysis aims to understand the phylogenetic relationships among these taxa, with a focus on the Elasmotheriinae clade. The data matrix used is based on Geraads et al. (2016), with modifications to improve accuracy.

steps.description: Using TNT (Goloboff and Catalano 2016), we conducted a parsimony analysis on 74 taxa of Old World rhinos (plus Tapirus terrestris as an outgroup), using the data matrix of Geraads et al. (2016), with some corrections, deletions of characters whose coding is too subjective, and addition of the few post-cranial characters that can be observed in Eoazara xerrii gen. et sp. nov. at Skoura.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct parsimony analysis using TNT software with modified data matrix
inputs: data matrix from Geraads et al. (2016)

steps.description: Using the default options of the 'Traditional search', followed by a second round of TBR, TNT yields 87 trees with a length of 1782 steps; the strict consensus tree has 1836 steps and is poorly resolved, but the majority rule consensus tree (cut-off 50) has 1784 steps only.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform traditional search and TBR in TNT to generate consensus trees

steps.description: Another analysis was run on the 59 taxa that have at least 50% of their characters scored. It yields 16 trees of 1630 steps, with a majority rule consensus tree (cut-off 50), of 1644 steps.
steps.searchable_summary: Run analysis on taxa with at least 50% characters scored to generate trees

steps.description: The most obvious result is that all taxa commonly included in the Elasmotheriinae form a clade.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify clade formation among Elasmotheriinae taxa

steps.description: Our results differ significantly from several previous analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare current analysis results with previous studies
